$comment: #1393 r3 Item 3 — closed registry of human-authored audit-tag verbs accepted in experiment.yaml as `[audit:<verb>=<value>]`. Producer = human (the user authoring the spec); consumer = lead + AST scanner. Skeleton for #1387 (forthcoming). Adding a verb: open a PR appending an entry here; the linter handler `audit_tag_verb_recognized` accepts the new verb on next run.
$contract: Each verb declares value_type (path | free-text | event-key | enum) and consumer (lead | ast-scanner). value_type=path values are interpreted as repo-relative URLs/routes for AST scanning. consumer=ast-scanner verbs are validated by audit_tag_claim_matches_ast handler.

api-fetch.description: Page declares it fetches this API path at runtime. AST scanner asserts the generated page source contains a matching fetch('<path>') call. Example: [audit:api-fetch=/api/portfolio]

seo.description: SEO claim attached to a page or behavior. Read by the lead during review; no AST enforcement. Example: [audit:seo=High-intent keyword]

event.description: Page or behavior declares it fires this analytics event. AST scanner asserts a matching trackEvent('<key>') or equivalent call exists in source. Example: [audit:event=signup_started]

sitemap-instance.description: #1387: page declares dynamic-segment URL instances must appear in sitemap.ts. Value format: '<route-prefix>/<segment-name>' (e.g., portfolio/slug). AST scanner asserts src/app/sitemap.ts iterates over <segment-name>. Behavior-verifier B7 in /verify additionally validates each declared dynamic_segments[<segment>][...] value appears in /sitemap.xml. Example: [audit:sitemap-instance=portfolio/slug]

ai-conversation.description: #1387: page declares a multi-turn AI conversation surface. AST scanner asserts the page contains BOTH a fetch('/api/...') call (any internal API route) AND useState/useReducer/useChat for turn state. Behavior-verifier B7 validates the API route receives real POSTs with non-stub bodies at runtime. Example: [audit:ai-conversation]

render.description: #1387: trivial 'page must render' claim. AST scanner skips (page existence is already checked at state-11c). Reserved for human-readable test prose. Example: [audit:render] page renders for known slug
